julia.executablePath: using PythonPlot  # ============================================================ # Kernel functions # ============================================================  function no_kernel(K::Integer)     return ones(Float64, K + 1) end  function jackson_kernel(K::Integer)     g = zeros(Float64, K + 1)     α = π / (K + 2)     cotα = cos(α) / sin(α)      for n in 0:K         g[n+1] = ((K - n + 1) * cos(n * α) + sin(n * α) * cotα) / (K + 2)     end      return g end  function exp_kernel(K::Integer; λ::Real=4.0)     g = zeros(Float64, K + 1)     for n in 0:K         g[n+1] = exp(-λ * n / K)     end     return g end  # ============================================================ # Raw Chebyshev coefficients of rectangular function on [-1,1] # rect = 1 on [a,b], 0 otherwise # ============================================================  function rect_cheb_coeffs_raw(a::Real, b::Real, K::Integer)     @assert -1 <= a < b <= 1 "Need -1 <= a < b <= 1"      θ1 = acos(b)     θ2 = acos(a)      c = zeros(Float64, K + 1)     c[1] = (θ2 - θ1) / π      for n in 1:K         c[n+1] = (2 / π) * (sin(n * θ2) - sin(n * θ1)) / n     end      return c end  function apply_kernel(c::AbstractVector, kernel::Function)     K = length(c) - 1     g = kernel(K)     @assert length(g) == length(c) "Kernel must return a vector of length K+1"     return c .* g end  # ============================================================ # Evaluate Chebyshev series on [-1,1] # ============================================================  function eval_cheb_series(c::AbstractVector, x::AbstractVector)     K = length(c) - 1     y = similar(x, Float64)      for (j, xx) in pairs(x)         if K == 0             y[j] = c[1]             continue         end          Tnm1 = 1.0         Tn   = xx         s = c[1] * Tnm1 + c[2] * Tn          for n in 2:K             Tnp1 = 2 * xx * Tn - Tnm1             s += c[n+1] * Tnp1             Tnm1, Tn = Tn, Tnp1         end          y[j] = s     end      return y end  # ============================================================ # Rectangle and Fourier transform # ============================================================  rect(x, a, b) = ((a <= x <= b) ? 1.0 : 0.0)  function rect_ft_exact(ω::AbstractVector, a::Real, b::Real)     F = Vector{ComplexF64}(undef, length(ω))     for (j, w) in pairs(ω)         if iszero(w)             F[j] = b - a         else             F[j] = (exp(-1im * w * a) - exp(-1im * w * b)) / (1im * w)         end     end     return F end  function numerical_ft(x::AbstractVector, f::AbstractVector, ω::AbstractVector)     F = Vector{ComplexF64}(undef, length(ω))     dx = x[2] - x[1]      for (j, w) in pairs(ω)         integrand = f .* exp.(-1im * w .* x)         F[j] = dx * (sum(integrand) - 0.5 * (integrand[1] + integrand[end]))     end      return F end  # ============================================================ # Main plotting function with rescaling # # scale = half-width of total x interval [-scale, scale] # rect_halfwidth = half-width of box inside that interval # # Internally use y = x / scale, so Chebyshev is on [-1,1] # ============================================================  function plot_rect_cheb_and_ft(;     scale=1.0,     rect_halfwidth=0.2,     K=50,     Nx=4000,     Nω=2000,     ωmax=200.0,     kernel::Function=no_kernel,     kernel_label::AbstractString="cheb" )     @assert scale > 0 "scale must be positive"     @assert 0 < rect_halfwidth < scale "Need 0 < rect_halfwidth < scale"      # physical interval: x in [-scale, scale]     x = collect(range(-scale, scale, length=Nx))      # rescaled interval: y in [-1,1]     y = x ./ scale      # rectangle in physical x-space: 1 on [-rect_halfwidth, rect_halfwidth]     f_exact = [rect(xx, -rect_halfwidth, rect_halfwidth) for xx in x]      # corresponding interval in rescaled y-space     a_scaled = -rect_halfwidth / scale     b_scaled =  rect_halfwidth / scale      c_raw = rect_cheb_coeffs_raw(a_scaled, b_scaled, K)     c = apply_kernel(c_raw, kernel)      # evaluate Chebyshev series in y = x/scale     f_cheb = eval_cheb_series(c, y)      ω = collect(range(-ωmax, ωmax, length=Nω))     F_cheb  = numerical_ft(x, f_cheb, ω)     F_exact = rect_ft_exact(ω, -rect_halfwidth, rect_halfwidth)      F_exact_abs = abs.(F_exact)     F_cheb_abs  = abs.(F_cheb)      F_exact_abs ./= maximum(F_exact_abs)     F_cheb_abs  ./= maximum(F_cheb_abs)      fig, ax = subplots(1, 2, figsize=(7.5, 3.5))     ax1 = ax[0]     ax2 = ax[1]      ax1.plot(x, f_exact, label="rect")     ax1.plot(x, f_cheb, label=kernel_label)     ax1.set_xlabel("x")     ax1.set_title("Rect / Cheb")     ax1.legend()     ax1.grid(true)      ax2.plot(ω, F_exact_abs, "--", label="exact")     ax2.plot(ω, F_cheb_abs, label=kernel_label)     ax2.set_xlabel("ω")     ax2.set_title("FT")     # ax2.set_xlim(-50, 50)     ax2.legend()     ax2.grid(true)      fig.tight_layout()      return fig, ax, (         x=x,         y=y,         ω=ω,         scale=scale,         rect_halfwidth=rect_halfwidth,         f_exact=f_exact,         f_cheb=f_cheb,         F_exact=F_exact,         F_cheb=F_cheb,         F_exact_abs=F_exact_abs,         F_cheb_abs=F_cheb_abs,         coeffs_raw=c_raw,         coeffs=c     ) end  # ============================================================ # Examples # ============================================================  # Original-type case on [-1,1] # fig, ax, data = plot_rect_cheb_and_ft( #     scale=1.0, #     rect_halfwidth=0.5, #     K=100, #     ωmax=300, #     kernel=jackson_kernel, #     kernel_label="jackson" # )  # Rescaled case on [-3,3] fig, ax, data = plot_rect_cheb_and_ft(     scale=25.0,     rect_halfwidth=10,     K=100,     ωmax=1,     kernel=jackson_kernel,     kernel_label="jackson" )  display(fig)
